title: Haystack AI Tool Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for using Haystack effectively in AI applications.

commands.description: Add a retriever node to the pipeline for fetching relevant documents.
commands.scenario: When setting up a pipeline to retrieve documents based on a query.

commands.description: Use Elasticsearch as the document store for efficient search and retrieval.
commands.scenario: When you need scalable and fast document retrieval capabilities.

commands.description: Retrieve the top 5 documents most relevant to the query.
commands.scenario: When you need to fetch a limited number of highly relevant documents.

commands.description: Use a reader to extract answers from the retrieved documents.
commands.scenario: When you need to extract precise answers from a set of documents.

commands.description: Execute the pipeline to process a query and return results.
commands.scenario: When you want to get answers from your document database using a pipeline.

commands.description: Enable GPU usage for faster processing of queries and training.
commands.scenario: When you have access to a GPU and need to speed up processing.

commands.description: Add a generator node to create answers from retrieved documents.
commands.scenario: When you need to generate answers rather than extract them.

commands.description: Set the batch size for processing to optimize memory usage.
commands.scenario: When tuning performance to fit within memory constraints.

commands.description: Write documents to the document store for future retrieval.
commands.scenario: When you need to add new documents to your search index.

commands.description: Add a summarizer node to condense information from documents.
commands.scenario: When you need to provide concise summaries of documents.

commands.description: Set the number of top documents to retrieve initially.
commands.scenario: When you need more documents for better answer generation.

commands.description: Add a classifier node to categorize documents or queries.
commands.scenario: When you need to classify documents for better organization.

commands.description: Disable the progress bar for cleaner log output in production.
commands.scenario: When running in production and you want to reduce log clutter.

commands.description: Add a translator node to handle multilingual queries and documents.
commands.scenario: When dealing with multilingual data and need translation.

commands.description: Visualizes the pipeline structure and connectivity to verify data flow between nodes before execution.
Overrides specific component parameters dynamically at runtime without re-initializing the pipeline.
Optimizes performance by performing incremental updates to the index instead of full re-indexing.
Serializes the entire pipeline architecture and component configurations for deployment or versioning.
Injects custom prompt-engineering patterns into Generator nodes to control output style and constraints.
Override specific component parameters dynamically during runtime without modifying the pipeline definition.
Pre-load model weights into GPU memory to prevent latency spikes during the first inference call.
Force the pipeline to return a dictionary of all component outputs instead of just the final node's result, useful for debugging intermediate steps.
Apply metadata-based pre-filtering to the document store to narrow the search space before retrieval.
Perform a structural check to ensure all input/output connections and data types are compatible before execution.
